description: Define the on-disk structure for incident artifacts: timeline.jsonl (chronological events), observations.jsonl (verified facts), hypotheses.md (current top hypotheses with confidence), actions.jsonl (actions taken or proposed), changelog.jsonl (deploys / config changes), runbook-execution.jsonl. Implement append helpers in src/incident/timeline.ts that every other Tier 3 sprint will use.

successCriteria.description: src/incident/types.ts defines: IncidentId (string), IncidentArtifactKind = 'timeline'|'observation'|'hypothesis'|'action'|'change'|'runbook-execution'|'diagnosis'|'postmortem', TimelineEvent shape, ObservationEntry shape, ActionEntry shape, ChangeEntry shape (with required inverse field for rollback).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read file. Verify each named type with documented fields.

successCriteria.description: src/incident/timeline.ts exports: createIncident(symptom): Promise<IncidentId> (creates .bober/incidents/<id>/ skeleton with all jsonl files initialized empty + a minimal incident.json metadata), appendTimeline(incidentId, event), appendObservation, appendAction, appendChange, appendRunbookExecution. All append helpers are atomic (rename-after-write or use fs.appendFile with mutex).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read file. Verify each export. Test: createIncident produces .bober/incidents/<id>/{incident.json,timeline.jsonl,observations.jsonl,actions.jsonl,changelog.jsonl,runbook-execution.jsonl,hypotheses.md,diagnoses/}.

successCriteria.description: IncidentId format: 'inc-<YYYYMMDD>-<short-slug>' where short-slug is derived from the symptom (first 3 words, kebab-case, max 30 chars).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Test: createIncident('500 errors on checkout endpoint') → returns 'inc-20260524-500-errors-on'.

successCriteria.description: TimelineEvent shape: {timestamp, eventKind, source ('diagnoser'|'deployer'|'human'|'observability'|'system'), summary (string), refPath? (path to detail artifact)}. Every other append helper ALSO writes a corresponding timeline event (e.g., appendAction writes both actions.jsonl and timeline.jsonl).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Test: appendAction({...}) results in both actions.jsonl AND timeline.jsonl gaining new lines.

successCriteria.description: ChangeEntry has REQUIRED inverse field: {id, type, executedAt, description, inverse: {description, command?}, status: 'pending'|'executed'|'rolled-back'|'failed'}. The inverse field is required at write time, NOT optional. Sprint 21 (rollback awareness) consumes this.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read ChangeEntry definition. Verify inverse is required. Test: appendChange without inverse throws schema error.

successCriteria.description: incident.json metadata shape: {incidentId, symptom, createdAt, status: 'investigating'|'remediating'|'monitoring'|'resolved'|'aborted', resolvedAt?, resolutionCriteria?, postmortemPath?}. Status updates via setIncidentStatus helper.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read shape. Verify all fields. Verify setIncidentStatus helper exists.

successCriteria.description: Listing helper: listIncidents(): Promise<IncidentSummary[]> reads .bober/incidents/*/incident.json and returns summaries sorted by createdAt desc.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Test: create 3 incidents, listIncidents returns 3 sorted entries.

successCriteria.description: Unit tests cover: createIncident, all five append helpers, listIncidents, setIncidentStatus. Run npm run test.

generatorNotes:
Use zod for schema validation on all append helpers. The ChangeEntry inverse-required constraint is the most important — sprint 21 (rollback awareness) needs every change to have an inverse declared at write time, NOT retrofitted later.

File creation atomicity: for jsonl appends, fs.appendFile with O_APPEND is atomic for small writes (<PIPE_BUF, ~4KB on most systems). Use the same mutex pattern from Sprint 13 audit.ts for safety on larger payloads.

Incident directory skeleton (createIncident output):
```
.bober/incidents/inc-20260524-500-errors-on/
  incident.json
  timeline.jsonl
  observations.jsonl
  actions.jsonl
  changelog.jsonl
  runbook-execution.jsonl
  hypotheses.md
  diagnoses/
```

The timeline.jsonl acts as the master event log — every change to incident state appends here too. This makes 'show me what happened in chronological order' a single file read.

ChangeEntry inverse examples (for skill body / documentation):
```typescript
// Scaling deployment up
{ type: 'k8s_scale', description: 'kubectl scale deployment api --replicas=6', inverse: { description: 'kubectl scale deployment api --replicas=3', command: 'kubectl scale deployment api --replicas=3' } }

// Feature flag flip
{ type: 'flag_toggle', description: 'Enabled new_checkout_flow=true', inverse: { description: 'Disable new_checkout_flow', command: 'curl ... -X PATCH ... -d {"enabled":false}' } }

// Database migration (more nuanced — sometimes inverse is 'run rollback migration')
{ type: 'db_migration', description: 'Applied migration 042', inverse: { description: 'Run rollback migration for 042', command: 'npm run migrate:rollback -- --to 041' } }
```

ResolveProjectRoot util (if not already in agent-bober's utils): walk up from cwd looking for bober.config.json or .bober/. Use across all incident path resolution.
evaluatorNotes:
Verify ChangeEntry inverse field is REQUIRED at the zod schema level. A test that calls appendChange without inverse MUST throw a clear error before the file is written. If the field is optional, sprint 21's rollback awareness fails silently when older changes have no inverse.

Verify the timeline.jsonl is updated by EVERY append helper, not just appendTimeline. Without this, 'show me what happened' requires reading 5 different files and merging by timestamp — defeats the timeline.

Verify atomicity: a unit test that crashes mid-append must leave the jsonl file with VALID lines only (no partial JSON). Use the rename-after-write pattern if the mutex approach doesn't guarantee this on the platform.

Verify file permissions follow Sprint 13's pattern (mode 0600 on creation) — incident artifacts may contain prod data; assume eventually-readable but minimize default exposure.

Verify the IncidentId slug derivation handles edge cases: empty symptom, all-punctuation symptom, unicode symptom. Tests should cover at least: regular text, Unicode chars, empty string (fall back to 'untitled'), very long string (truncate at 30 chars).

Verify listIncidents handles missing .bober/incidents/ directory gracefully — return empty array, no crash.
